claim: For all integers n, gcd(n^2 + 1, n + 1) divides 2.
For all integers n, n^2 + 1 is never divisible by 4.
For all integers n, gcd(n^3 - n, n+1) divides 3.
For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 6 if n is odd.
For all integers n, n^4 + 4 is never prime for |n|>1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^5 - n, n+1) divides 5.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 30 for all integers n.
For all integers n, n^4 + n^2 + 1 is coprime with n+1.
For all integers n, n^3 - n and n^5 - n share the same set of prime divisors.
predicate: set([p for p in range(2,abs(n)+2) if n>1 and (n**3-n)%p==0])==set([p for p in range(2,abs(n)+2) if (n**5-n)%p==0])

For all integers n, n^4+4 divides n^12-1.

For all integers n, n^2+1, n^3-n, n^5-n are pairwise coprime when n is prime.
